initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of inferior myocardial infarction?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose inferior myocardial infarction, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment elevation in leads V1 and V2
Presence of ST-segment elevation in inferior leads
Presence of diffuse concave ST-segment elevation in limb leads
Presence of PR-segment depression in inferior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment elevation in inferior leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST-segment elevation in at least two of the inferior leads, including leads II, III, and aVF?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have inferior myocardial infarction?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose inferior myocardial infarction?
options: Presence of a Delta wave (slurred upstroke) in inferior leads
Presence of fragmented QRS complexes in inferior leads
Presence of QS complexes in leads V1 and V2
Presence of pathological Q waves in inferior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of pathological Q waves in inferior leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show pathological Q waves in at least two of the inferior leads, including leads II, III, and aVF? Note that a pathological Q wave in these leads is defined as (1) a Q wave with a duration greater than 30 milliseconds and an amplitude greater than one-fifth of the following R amplitude, or (2) a deep S wave with only a small R wave preceding it.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have inferior myocardial infarction?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
